# OpenAI-compatible production troubleshooting and API Doctor

> Operational reference for turning ambiguous API failures into reproducible evidence.

## Scope

This repository is for diagnosis after or before client integration. It intentionally does not maintain a model catalog or duplicate Cursor, Dify, Claude Code and Codex configuration tables.

Examples use `https://www.aifast.club/v1`, but API Doctor accepts any OpenAI-compatible Base URL. Provider identity is not part of its pass/fail logic.

## Diagnostic sequence

1. Normalize the Base URL and reject malformed targets.
2. Call `GET /models` to separate authentication, catalog and rate-limit failures.
3. Optionally call `POST /chat/completions` with a fixed expected output.
4. Record HTTP status, request ID, response model, elapsed time and visible usage.
5. Produce a redacted JSON report with an error-specific next action.

## Error decisions

- DNS/TLS: check hostname, certificate, system time and network egress.
- 401/403: check the bearer key, key scope and account state; never publish the key.
- 404/model not found: copy the exact current model ID and check duplicated `/v1` paths.
- 429: apply bounded exponential backoff with jitter and retain retry headers.
- 5xx/timeout: retry only safe operations, cap attempts and keep request IDs.
- HTTP 200 with unexpected content: validate Content-Type, JSON shape, model claim and output assertion.

## Failover boundary

Route failover keeps the requested model while changing an upstream path. Model fallback changes the requested model and can alter tools, image support, context and output shape. Cross-model fallback must be an explicit, tested application policy.

## Security properties

- API keys come from environment variables.
- A plaintext `--api-key` argument is not supported.
- Reports and upstream error bodies are recursively redacted.
- Real keys belong in CI Secrets and should not be exposed to external-fork workflows.

## Related evidence

- API Doctor source and tests: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/llm-api-proxy-china
- Online protocol and model check: https://docs.aifast.club/model-check/
- Model-check report interpretation: https://docs.aifast.club/guides/model-check-report-guide/
- Task-based start: https://docs.aifast.club/start/
- Stability statistics: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/AI-API-Stability-Tracker
- Client integration: https://github.com/KKWANG4444/ai-api-proxy-china-guide
- AIFast first-party product facts: https://kkwang4444.github.io/api-status/brand-facts/

Current model IDs, maintenance state, prices and account rules must be verified in the live console. This repository does not duplicate volatile catalog data.
